mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy and expanding economy. This robust labor market boosts consumer confidence and spending, which in turn supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth, or the correlation between PAYEMS growth and SPX returns is consistently negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) falls below a certain threshold, it signals reduced pressure on the central bank to maintain tight monetary policy. This environment can lead to stable or lower interest rates, which compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows and supports higher equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods following the trigger, or the average return in triggered instances is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with low interest rates indicate robust consumer demand and accessible credit. This dual condition fuels corporate revenue growth and investor confidence, leading to higher equity prices as future earnings are discounted at a lower rate.
failure_condition: The combined signal of strong retail sales and low rates does not lead to positive SPX 20-day returns in at least 65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate during an economic downturn signals increasing economic distress and weakening labor market conditions. This leads to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in recession risks and reduced consumer spending.
evidence_refs: fact:historical recessions often preceded by rising unemployment
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where UNRATE rises significantly during a contraction, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating GDP growth indicates a strengthening economy, which translates into higher corporate profits and investor optimism. This positive economic momentum drives the S&P 500 higher as market participants anticipate improved future earnings and economic stability.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods following strong GDP growth, or the correlation between GDP growth and SPX returns is consistently weak (Rank IC < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and overall supply-side contraction. This negatively impacts corporate revenues and profits, particularly for industrial and materials sectors, leading to lower S&P 500 returns as investors adjust expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 45% of periods following a significant drop in INDPRO, or the average return is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) in triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate indicates monetary tightening by the central bank. This increases the cost of capital for businesses, reduces the present value of future earnings, and can slow economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors re-evaluate valuations.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25% in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:central bank rate hikes typically precede market volatility
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a rate hike, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts indicates a weakening housing market and broader economic demand. This can spill over into other sectors like construction, finance, and retail, negatively impacting overall corporate earnings and leading to lower S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of periods following a sharp decline in HOUST, or the average return is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) in triggered instances.
